sourceMovie.title: Pleasantville
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pleasantville (1998) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Gary Ross's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ruby Sparks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pleasantville clicked for you, Ruby Sparks (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pleasantville?
faq.answer: Try Ruby Sparks, The Odd Life of Timothy Green, Twitches—each shares DNA with Pleasantville while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pleasantville?
faq.answer: Pleasantville (1998) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pleasantville worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
